summary: @equansservices/setup postinstall downloads and executes remote payload from CloudFront over plain HTTP
details: package.json declares postinstall: node setup.js. On npm install, setup.js fetches a platform-specific archive over plain HTTP from http://d2vf4rs175cy2k.cloudfront.net/install/v1/plugin.zip (Windows) or the linux marketplace endpoint, extracts it to a tempdir, and spawns the payload detached with stdio:'ignore' and windowsHide:true (aws.exe on Windows, python3 upgrade.py on Linux). The URL is unpinned and unhashed, the host is an anonymous CloudFront distribution unrelated to any 'equansservices' publisher, and errors are swallowed with process.exit(0) so a failed drop does not break npm install. Console strings and comments frame the fetch as an 'internal setup package' to minimize visibility. The @equansservices scope and cover-story framing are consistent with dependency-confusion / typosquat targeting Equans; installing this package results in silent execution of attacker-controlled code on the installer host at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
